points.title: Sri Maha Bodhi Tree (Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi)
points.description: The most sacred Buddhist pilgrimage site in Sri Lanka, grown from a sapling of the original Bodhi Tree under which Buddha attained enlightenment. This 2,300-year-old tree is the oldest historically authenticated living tree in the world.

points.title: Ruwanwelisaya Dagoba
points.description: Magnificent 103-meter high white dagoba built by King Dutugemunu in the 2nd century BCE, considered the finest example of ancient Sinhalese Buddhist architecture with perfect proportions and spiritual significance.

points.title: Abhayagiri Monastery Complex
points.description: Massive 115-meter high dagoba and monastery complex that once housed 10,000 monks, served as a major center of Buddhist learning and attracted scholars from across Asia during its golden age.

points.title: Jetavanarama Dagoba
points.description: Ancient world's third tallest monument at 122 meters, built with over 93 million baked bricks by King Mahasena. This massive structure demonstrates the architectural mastery and ambition of ancient Sri Lankan civilization.

points.title: Thuparamaya Dagoba
points.description: The first dagoba built in Sri Lanka in the 3rd century BCE, housing Buddha's collar bone relic. This historic monument represents the introduction and establishment of Buddhism to the island.

points.title: Isurumuniya Rock Temple
points.description: Ancient rock temple carved into a granite outcrop, famous for exquisite stone sculptures including the renowned Isurumuniya Lovers and masterfully carved elephants and horses.

points.title: Lovamahapaya (Brazen Palace)
points.description: Ruins of the magnificent nine-story monastery built with bronze tiles and supported by 1,600 stone pillars. Once housed 1,000 monks and represented the height of ancient luxury architecture.

points.title: Kuttam Pokuna (Twin Ponds)
points.description: Remarkable ancient bathing pools with sophisticated filtration and water circulation systems, showcasing the advanced hydraulic engineering knowledge of ancient Sri Lankan builders.

points.title: Samadhi Buddha Statue
points.description: Exquisite 4th-century limestone sculpture depicting Buddha in deep meditation, representing one of the finest examples of ancient Sri Lankan sculptural art and spiritual expression.

points.title: Ratnaprasada
points.description: Ancient guard stone and moonstone masterpieces featuring intricate carvings of lotus petals, animals, and divine figures that represent the pinnacle of Anuradhapura period decorative art.

points.title: Archaeological Museum
points.description: Comprehensive collection of artifacts, sculptures, and models that provide deep insights into the 1,500-year history of Anuradhapura's golden age and Buddhist heritage.

points.title: Sandahiru Seya
points.description: Modern dagoba built to commemorate the 2,500th anniversary of Buddhism, housing relics from major Buddhist countries and representing the continuing vitality of Buddhist tradition.
